content: Core-satellite portfolio construction combines passive and active strategies. The core (60-80% of assets) is passively invested in broad market indices, capturing beta at low cost. Satellites (20-40%) are actively managed positions targeting alpha through: sector bets, factor tilts, alternative strategies, or individual stock picks. This hybrid approach balances efficiency with flexibility: core provides diversification and low fees, satellites allow tactical views without jeopardizing overall portfolio integrity. Institutional investors widely use this structure. Key consideration: satellite allocations should have clear theses and risk limits; otherwise they become closet indexing with higher fees. Rebalancing between core and satellites maintains target allocations.
